items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith to attend first Anzac Day service since war crime charges
items.standfirst: Former soldier says he will ‘pay my respects’ at commemoration in Queensland where he has been living since being released on bailGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastBen Roberts-Smith will attend an Anzac Day service in Queensland on Saturday morning, describing the day as “sacred” to him, the first commemoration since he was criminally charged.Roberts-Smith, the recipient of the Victoria Cross and once one of Australia’s most lionised soldiers, faces five charges of the war crime of murder, allegedly committed during his service with the SAS in Afghanistan between 2009 and 2012. Continue reading...
items.content: Former soldier says he will ‘pay my respects’ at commemoration in Queensland where he has been living since being released on bailGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastBen Roberts-Smith will attend an Anzac Day service in Queensland on Saturday morning, describing the day as “sacred” to him, the first commemoration since he was criminally charged.Roberts-Smith, the recipient of the Victoria Cross and once one of Australia’s most lionised soldiers, faces five charges of the war crime of murder, allegedly committed during his service with the SAS in Afghanistan between 2009 and 2012. Continue reading...

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith confirms Anzac Day plans
items.standfirst: Ben Roberts-Smith confirms he will be attending Anzac Day services tomorrow, saying he intends to pay his respects, one week on from his release on bail.
items.content: Ben Roberts-Smith confirms he will be attending Anzac Day services tomorrow, saying he intends to pay his respects, one week on from his release on bail.

clusters.title: What happens after you buy a house?
clusters.neutral_headline: What happens after you buy a house

items.title: What happens after you buy a house?
items.standfirst: You've heard it all before: it's harder to buy a house. But, if you do manage to get your foot on the property ladder, things are also more difficult afterwards. The housing crisis has turned into an intergenerational wealth crisis, set to make paying your mortgage in retirement the norm. So how did we get there? And how do we fix it?
items.content: You've heard it all before: it's harder to buy a house. But, if you do manage to get your foot on the property ladder, things are also more difficult afterwards. The housing crisis has turned into an intergenerational wealth crisis, set to make paying your mortgage in retirement the norm. So how did we get there? And how do we fix it?
